meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve no marketing skills (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome no marketing skills. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no marketing' barrier by building a high-impact portfolio. Master Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first SaaS project as a student.
content.answer_block: You don't need a marketing budget; you need a proof-of-work portfolio. By building high-utility AI tools using Antigravity and Claude Code, you demonstrate technical competency that speaks louder than any ad. Your code becomes your marketing engine, attracting recruiters and users through tangible, live-deployed value that proves your engineering capability.
content.intro:
The screen glows with the cursor blinking—a silent, mocking reminder of the 'no marketing' trap. You have the technical skills, but the prospect of navigating complex customer acquisition funnels feels like a mountain you aren't ready to climb. You feel invisible in a sea of noise, wondering how to bridge the gap between 'code student' and 'shipped builder.'

Stop trying to be a marketer and start being a builder. The most effective way to solve the marketing problem is to replace it with a portfolio of live, functional AI applications. When your work is visible, verifiable, and solving actual problems, the marketing happens automatically through the weight of your results.

steps.title: Define a High-Utility Micro-SaaS
steps.description: Identify a repetitive, manual task in a niche community and design a one-click AI solution. Focus on utility over complexity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Suggest 3 high-utility micro-SaaS ideas that solve a specific problem for students, focused on automation, and provide a technical architecture for the simplest version (MVP).
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review these 3 micro-SaaS ideas and recommend the one with the highest potential for a portfolio showcase.'

steps.title: Initialize the Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a production-ready boilerplate. This ensures your project follows industry standards from line one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern Next.js boilerplate with Tailwind CSS, Supabase authentication, and an AI integration layer using the OpenAI SDK. Include a clean directory structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a new Next.js project based on the Antigravity architecture provided.'

steps.title: Implement the Core AI Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write, test, and refine the core AI function. Focus on edge-case handling and clean API interactions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust API route for an AI agent that takes user input and returns structured JSON. Include error handling for token limits and rate limiting.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Implement the core AI logic into the API route and add unit tests for success and failure states.'

steps.title: Build the User Interface
steps.description: Create a seamless interface that hides the complexity of the AI backend. The UX should be intuitive and fast.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a minimal, high-conversion UI for the AI tool. Include a clean input field, a loading state with progress indicators, and a clear output display component.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Refactor the UI components to match the design specifications and ensure responsive layout.'

steps.title: Deploy and Document
steps.description: Deploy your project to Vercel and write a 'Build Log' that explains the 'Why' behind your code. This is your marketing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional 'Build Log' for a technical blog post. Explain the technical challenges solved, the choice of stack, and the impact of the tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a deployment summary and a README file that highlights the technical decision-making process.'

pro_tips: Always focus on the 'Build Log'—recruiters care more about how you solved the bug than the app itself.
Use 'Claude Code' to refactor your own code after finishing a feature; it teaches you cleaner patterns in real-time.
Don't build a generic to-do list; build a tool that automates a task you actually perform every day.
Keep your repository public and clean; it is your digital resume in the eyes of hiring managers.
Prioritize speed of iteration over perfection; a 'good enough' deployed app beats a 'perfect' local project.
content.next_steps: After completing your first project, iterate on the feedback loop by sharing it on X or LinkedIn. Take the lessons learned from the first build and apply them to a more complex project, focusing on integrating external APIs to expand the functionality of your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect moment to market yourself. Build the proof, own the narrative, and let your code do the heavy lifting. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
